rules:
This card can only be attached to a Single Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Single Strike Pokémon, discard this card.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Fighting and Darkness Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time, and the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

This card can only be attached to a Rapid Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Rapid Strike Pokémon, discard this card.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides 2 in any combination of Water Energy and Fighting Energy.

Search your deck for a Pokémon with 90 HP or less, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, you may move a Basic Energy from that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

flavorText: Identifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.
It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

fullDisplayName: Single Strike Style Mustard 177/163 Battle Styles
rules:
You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

This card can only be attached to a Single Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Single Strike Pokémon, discard this card.

Shuffle up to 2 Single Strike Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

The Retreat Cost of each Rapid Strike Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) is ColorlessColorless less.

This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Once during each player's turn, that player may draw 2 cards. In order to use this effect, that player must discard a Single Strike card from their hand.

This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Hand Kinesis
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each card in your hand.

flavorText: Through its horns, it can pick up on the emotions of creatures around it. Positive emotions are the source of its strength.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Spiral Rush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it's angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

attacks.name: Head Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

attacks.name: Double Dip Fangs
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Caturday
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards. If you do, this Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.
When it's happy, Glameow demonstrates beautiful movements of its tail, like a dancing ribbon.

attacks.name: Selickt
attacks.effect: Your opponent chooses to discard the top 3 cards of their deck or to discard 3 cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Pitch
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses its tongue much more skillfully than its hands or its feet. It can deftly pick up a single small bean with its tongue.
It licks filth clean with its tongue. Whatever it licks always stinks afterward, so whether it's really clean is…questionable.
Carrying food through Fearow's territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!
Due to its short wings, it can't fly long distances. It wanders about restlessly and pecks at bug Pokémon.

attacks.name: G-Max Hurricane
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use G-Max Hurricane.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Sky Hurricane
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Sky Hurricane.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gigaton Bash
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.

flavorText: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.

attacks.name: Full Metal Blade
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Metal Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Once upon a time, a king with an Aegislash reigned over the land. His Pokémon eventually drained him of life, and his kingdom fell with him.

attacks.name: Cut Down
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The blue eye on the sword's handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people's lives away.

attacks.name: Steel Slice
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Metal Pokémon, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Violent conflicts erupt between Bisharp and Fraxure over places where sharpening stones can be found.

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Slash attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A pack of these Pokémon forms to serve a Bisharp boss. Each Pawniard trains diligently, dreaming of one day taking the lead.
Many scientists suspect that this Pokémon originated outside the Galar region, based on the patterns on its body.
Polishing Bronzor to a shine makes its surface reflect the truth, according to common lore. Be that as it may, Bronzor hates being polished.

attacks.name: Powerful Vise
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Piercing Strike
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

attacks.name: Steel Swing
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

attacks.name: Gather Food
attacks.effect: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Hangry Tackle
attacks.effect: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Hunger hormones affect its temperament. Until its hunger is appeased, it gets up to all manner of evil deeds.

attacks.name: Cragalanche
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Single Strike Crush
attacks.effect: Discard the top 4 cards of your deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Identifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.
They make repeated eerie howls before dawn to call attention to their pack.

attacks.name: Voltage Dive
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Its goons take care of most of the fighting for it. The only time it dirties its own hands is in delivering a final blow to finish off an opponent.

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: It searches for shiny things for its boss. Murkrow's presence is said to be unlucky, so many people detest it.

attacks.name: Splattering Poison
attacks.effect: Both Active Pokémon are now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Unhinged Hammer
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: If this Pokémon squeezes the tongue of the Shellder biting it, the Shellder will launch a toxic liquid from the tip of its shell.
It feeds on the blood of living people and Pokémon. If it can't drink any blood for even a short while, it becomes weak and unable to fly.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: They can suck down over 10 ounces of blood in one go. They have been known to drink so much blood that they can no longer fly.

attacks.name: Hide in Shadows
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Their skin is so thin that they'll be burned if sunlight hits them. When it gets cold out, they gather together to warm one another's bodies.

attacks.effect: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Fighting Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Impact Blow
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Impact Blow.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Land's Pulse
attacks.effect: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Hammer
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Giga Hammer.

flavorText: Once a year, on a specific date and at a specific time, they gather out of nowhere and form up in a circle.

attacks.name: Rapid Strike Squad
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Rapid Strike Pokémon in play.

flavorText: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

attacks.name: Big Sand Cannon
attacks.effect: Discard the top 6 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 damage for each Fighting Energy card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.
It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.

attacks.name: Coal Cannon
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: While it's engaged in battle, its mountain of coal will burn bright red, sending off sparks that scorch the surrounding area.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: By rapidly rolling its legs, it can travel at over 18 mph. The temperature of the flames it breathes exceeds 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can race around like a unicycle, even on rough, rocky terrain. Burning coal sustains it.

attacks.name: Spinning Whip
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

flavorText: When Mienshao lets out a bizarre wail, you're in danger. A flurry of kicks and chops too fast to see is about to be unleashed!

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: They seclude themselves in the mountains and devote themselves to training. The form of their kicks and chops differs from pack to pack.

attacks.name: Hammer Pressure
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: When going all out, this Pokémon throws aside its concrete pillars and leaps at opponents to pummel them with its fists.
Gurdurr excels at demolition—construction is not its forte. In any case, there's skill in the way this Pokémon wields its metal beam.
Timburr that have started carrying logs that are about three times their size are nearly ready to evolve.

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey… Then its elongated fangs do the rest.
It flies straight at its target's face then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

attacks.name: Bonemerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

flavorText: They thump their bones rhythmically to communicate among themselves. There are nearly 50 different rhythmic patterns.
It wears its mother's skull on its head, so no one knows what its bare face looks like. However, it's clear that it's always crying.

attacks.name: Rocky Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

attacks.name: Field Crush
attacks.effect: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it.

attacks.name: Steamin' Mad Strike
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

flavorText: The blood vessels in its brain are sturdier than those of other Pokémon, so it can stay healthy despite its constant raging.

attacks.name: Focus Fist
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: If one gets angry, all the others around it will get angry, so silence is a rare visitor in a troop of Mankey.

attacks.name: Evomancy
attacks.effect: For each Energy attached to this Pokémon, search your deck for a Stage 2 Pokémon, except Orbeetle, and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what's around it—and what's around it can include things over six miles away.

attacks.name: Radar
attacks.effect: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

attacks.name: Prismatic Ray
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Special Laser
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jealous Eyes
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: The defensive instinct of the males is strong. It's when they're protecting themselves or their partners that they unleash their full power.

attacks.name: Focused Wish
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: There's enough psychic power in Espurr to send a wrestler flying, but because this power can't be controlled, Espurr finds it troublesome.

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Pleasant Tone
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.

attacks.name: Power Split
attacks.effect: Attach Psychic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like until your Pokémon and your opponent's Pokémon have the same total amount of Energy attached.

flavorText: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.

attacks.name: Self-Destruct
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

attacks.name: Dazzle Dance
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does an odd dance to gain control over foes.
It bounces around on its tail to keep its heart pumping. It carries a pearl from Clamperl on its head.

attacks.name: Tantailizing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Because Galarian Slowpoke eat the seeds of a plant that grows only in Galar, their tails have developed a spicy flavor.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Defiant Spark
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack can be used for Lightning.

flavorText: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.
This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

attacks.name: Max Shock
attacks.effect: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, their Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spiral Thunder
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

attacks.name: Electrostep
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scar Strikes
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

attacks.name: Jumping Kick
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.
All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Lightning Slam
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Lightning Slam.

flavorText: A single Electivire can provide enough electricity for all the buildings in a big city for a year.

attacks.name: Haymaker
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Haymaker.

flavorText: While it's often blamed for power outages, the truth is the cause of outages is more often an error on the part of the electric company.
Its skin is thick enough to fend off Mareanie's spikes. With its robust teeth, Bruxish crunches up the spikes and eats them.

attacks.name: Sediment Sink
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each Water Energy card in your discard pile.

flavorText: Whenever a full moon hangs in the night sky, schools of Jellicent gather near the surface of the sea, waiting for their prey to appear.

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.

flavorText: Legend has it that the residents of a sunken ancient city changed into these Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swirling Slice
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: A brutish Pokémon that loves to battle. It will crash itself into any foe that approaches its nest.

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It was originally a Pokémon from afar that escaped to the wild. It can adapt to the dirtiest river.
It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.
Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine's underside to scavenge for leftovers.

attacks.name: Ball Juggling
attacks.effect: Discard any number of Item cards that have the word "Ball" in their name from your hand. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Its amusing movements make it very popular. It releases its psychic power from the pattern on its belly.

attacks.name: Find It
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It can radiate chilliness from the bottoms of its feet. It'll spend the whole day tap-dancing on a frozen floor.

attacks.name: Aqua Burst
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life forms can survive.
It is capable of swimming backwards by rapidly flapping its winglike pectoral fins and stout tail.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

attacks.name: Bursting Inferno
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they're well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Derisive Roasting
attacks.effect: This attack does 90 damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The winner of competitions between Salazzle is decided by which one has the most male Salandit with it.

attacks.name: Call Sign
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This sneaky Pokémon will slink behind its prey and immobilize it with poisonous gas before the prey even realizes Salandit is there.

attacks.name: Burning Licks
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.
It has mastered fast and powerful fighting moves. It grows a beard of fire.
When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.
It loves to eat roasted berries, but sometimes it gets too excited and burns them to a crisp.

attacks.name: Spreading Flames
attacks.effect: Attach up to 3 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Max Victory
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 120 more damage.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: V Bullet
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Flare Shot
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Heat Dash
attacks.effect: You may attach a Fire Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

attacks.name: G-Max Rolling
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sour Spit
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost ColorlessColorless more.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Nature's Judgment
attacks.effect: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Although it's called a guardian deity, it's violent enough to crush anyone it sees as an enemy.

attacks.name: Leaf Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: As it slashes into its enemies, it looks like it's dancing. Its elegant appearance has led some to call it the most glamorous Grass Pokémon.

attacks.name: Continuous Slash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It loves the sunshine. When it basks in the sunlight regularly, it grows up with vivid coloration.

attacks.name: Vital Powder
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from 2 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The patterns on this Pokémon's wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

attacks.name: Grass Cocooning
attacks.effect: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The beaks of birds can't begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

attacks.name: Devour
attacks.effect: For each of your Durant in play, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Triple Whip
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.
The faint scent that emanates from its full blossom entices bug Pokémon to it.
The deeper a Cherubi's red, the more nutrients it has stockpiled in its body. And the sweeter and tastier its small ball!

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pull
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spiny Punch
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.
It prefers harsh environments such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

attacks.name: Panic Vine
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Once ingested into this Pokémon's body, even the hardest object will melt into nothing.
It spits out Poison Powder to immobilize the enemy and then finishes it with a spray of Acid.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Its bud looks like a human face. Because of the bud, it is rumored to be a type of legendary mandrake plant.
